steps.title: Find the radius
steps.content:
Use the circumference formula for the circular base. The circumference is $18\pi$, so solve for the radius:
$$18\pi=2\pi r$$
$$r=9$$

steps.title: Use the volume formula
steps.content:
Substitute $r=9$ and $h=7$ into the cylinder volume formula, then multiply:
$$V=\pi(9)^2(7)=\pi\cdot81\cdot7=567\pi$$
The answer is C.
